answer:
The passage appears in *Bang-E-Dara Part 2* by Allama Iqbal, Chapter 16 ("Kali").[^1]

> It sips on splendor in the tavern of morn,  
> Its life from the Sun's own goblet is born.[^2]  
> 
> Before the Sun, it tears its breast apart,  
> What blissful pleasure for its tiny heart![^3]  
> 
> ...  
> 
> Let me behold my Sun, though far it seems,  
> And like a bud, be wrapped within its beams.[^4]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, [Chapter 16](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/16-kali/translation/).

[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/16-kali/translation/#p-ae86d8).

[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/16-kali/translation/#p-99fffa).

[^4]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/16-kali/translation/#p-44ef90).
